text: A nyaaŋa, o ko pori n ti ba o ziizii maa, dindaari yiiŋu maa. Dabso ti mari u moora, halli bee ti kaa.
translated: The child, if you don't teach him when he is young, when he is still small. When he grows up, there is nothing you can do.

ref: and now I own cattle, donkeys, and sheep, as well as many slaves. Master, I am sending these messengers in the hope that you will be kind to me.”
text: Ni o marini naagi, buŋsi, peeri, buusi, a ni gurunsi dabsi ni pogiba, ka o suusira i maa e zo u nimbaa.
translated: When you go to the market, buy meat, beans, rice, and groundnut soup ingredients, then cook them so that we can eat in the evening.

ref: Jacob's men took the gifts on ahead of him, but he spent the night in camp. Jacob's Name Is Changed to Israel
text: A zu, o ti ni piini ŋa pa yiya, ka ba do nisaaniŋi. Seema Yakubu ni Naawunni n daa n mo n ŋa
translated: And you, if you don't want to go there, then don't be surprised. Because Yakubu and Naawunni have already gone there

ref: Jacob said, “Now tell me your name.” “Don't you know who I am?” he asked. And he blessed Jacob.
text: Yakubu yeli u, “Wusi ma e yuuri.” “Bo zu n na e kpabira e baŋŋi m yuuri?” Ka nafa Yakubu.
translated: Yakubu said, "Give me water." "Why should I give you water for free?" asked the woman.

ref: When Jacob saw them, he said, “This is God's camp.” So he named the place Mahanaim.
text: O n si n nye ba, o yeli, “Naawunni ziiziya n ŋa.” Ka digili niŋi yuuri Mahanayim.
translated: When he saw them, he said, "This is the camp of God." So he called the name of that place Mahanaim.

ref: Jacob sent messengers on ahead to Esau, who lived in the land of Seir, also known as Edom.
text: Niŋi n na, Yakubu tumi o tumusi, ba pa yii we o beeri Isawu saani, a yi Seya n ba Idom paaligu.
translated: After that, Jacob lived in the land where his father Isaac had stayed, in the land of Canaan which is the Edomites' country.

ref: Afterwards, Jacob went back and spent the rest of the night alone. A man came and fought with Jacob until just before daybreak.
text: A nyaaŋa, o ko pori n ti ba o ziizii maa, dindaari yiiŋu maa. Dabso ti mari u moora, halli bee ti kaa.
translated: The child, if you do not teach him when he is young, when he grows up, it will be difficult, even if you want to do it.

ref: The man said, “From now on, your name will no longer be Jacob. You will be called Israel, because you have wrestled with God and with men, and you have won.”
text: Niŋi n na, daba maa yeli u, “Za zinaa wara, e yuuri bi lee n niŋi Yakubu, e yuuri n niŋi Izireel, a kpali bo? E ni Naawunni ni niriba n mo, e nyaŋŋi ba la zu.”
translated: And now, the time has come when, "The scepter will not depart from Judah, nor the ruler's staff from between his feet, until he comes to whom it belongs, and the obedience of the nations shall be his."

ref: Esau asked Jacob, “Why did you send those herds I met along the road?” “Master,” Jacob answered, “I sent them so you would be friendly to me.”
text: Isawu boosi u, “Ka buŋkobseesi ni tuusi la mini, bo n na a mi wusi?” Yakubu yeli u, “M yidaana, e piini n na, m nye seema ti mabiilim di n gbaa taaba.”
translated: Esau said to him, "Why do you deceive me and take what is mine, when you know that I am your elder brother?" Jacob said to him, "My lord, if you agree, I will give you my birthright so that you may have the blessing."

ref: Three days later the men who had been circumcised were still weak from pain. So Simeon and Levi, two of Dinah's brothers, attacked with their swords and killed every man in the town,
text: Dabaa ata nyaaŋa, jemanso niriba maa zaa ni ba beerim niŋi, Yakubu biduusi bayi, Simiyon ni Livai, Dayina sunzodabisi naa ba tokobisi kpe tiŋŋa maa puwa, seema niriba maa ni bi fe, a ku ba dabsi zaa nyem.
translated: Two days later, when all the men were still in pain, Jacob's sons, Simeon and Levi, Dinah's brothers, took their swords and attacked the town while the men were helpless, and they killed all the males.

ref: including Hamor and Shechem. Then they took Dinah and left.
text: Ba ku Hamo ni o bii Sekem, ka kpe Sekem dii mari Dayina yi n gaa.
translated: They came to Hamor and to his son Shechem, and Shechem spoke to Dinah's father.

ref: They took sheep, goats, donkeys, and everything else that was in the town or the countryside.
text: Ba gbaa ba peeri, buusi, naagi, a ni buŋsi ni bunso zaa n ba tiŋŋa maa puwa, a ni ba puuri niŋi.
translated: They will cut them, cook them, eat them, and their bones and everything that is in their bodies will be thrown away, and they will finish with them.

ref: Jacob went to his father Isaac at Hebron, also called Mamre or Kiriath-Arba, where Isaac's father Abraham had lived as a foreigner.
text: Niŋi n na, Yakubu pori n gaa o ba Yisaaka saani, a yi Mamiri, a leeli Kiriyaf-Aba seela Ibrahim ni Yisaaka n daa n be.
translated: When Jacob came to his father Isaac at Mamre, near Kiriath-arba where Abraham and Isaac had lived.

ref: One day, Joseph told his brothers what he had dreamed, and they hated him even more.
text: Dindaso, Yisifu zaasi zaasim, ka togsi o yela ti o beya maa, ba chisi u ga paasi.
translated: When Joseph's brothers saw that their father was dead, they became afraid.

ref: Reuben heard this and tried to protect Joseph from them. “Let's not kill him,” he said.
text: Rubin n wumi n ŋa, o niŋi aniya, seema o n di n niŋi n moligi biyaa yi o beya ŋa saani. Ka yeli ba, “Ti da ku u.
translated: Rubin heard this, and he wanted to save him, so that he could return him to his father. So he said, "We should not kill him."

ref: When Reuben returned to the well and did not find Joseph there, he tore his clothes in sorrow.
text: Niŋi n na, Rubin daa n lee n pori ta bulugu maa ziya, ka bi nye Yisifu yi o puwa, sufusaaŋu gbaag u, o darigi o bimpinna kasigi.
translated: When Reuben returned to the pit and found that Joseph was not in the pit, he tore his clothes in grief.

ref: Meanwhile, the Midianites had sold Joseph in Egypt to a man named Potiphar, who was the king's official in charge of the palace guard.
text: Jemanso yawdiriba la n daa n da Yisifu maa, ba mari u gaa Yijipiti paaligu, a koosi u ti daba yindaana, ba n boona Potifa. Dabaa daa n niŋini Yijipiti paaligu zaa naaba guuriba la kpeema.
translated: When his brothers sold Joseph, they took him to the land of Egypt, and he became a slave there, they were calling him Potiphar. The slave was serving the king of all the land of Egypt.

ref: and Potiphar's wife soon noticed him. She asked him to make love to her,
text: A n ti paa jemani yinni, o yidaana poga nyaasi u, ka yeli u, “Ti mari ma do.”
translated: When they reached the place, the chief's son greeted him, and said to him, "We have come to you."

ref: She kept begging Joseph day after day, but he refused to do what she wanted or even to go near her.
text: Bee zaa, o yetirini Yisifu ni o mari u do, o mi zagsi, ka bi kpabira o kpatira u.
translated: When they saw Joseph coming from afar, before he reached them, they conspired to kill him.

ref: and he asked, “Why are you so worried today?”
text: Niŋi n na, o boosi ba, “Bo n mari ya, ya dugi nufusi zinaa lala?”
translated: Then he asked them, "What have you done, have you killed all the animals?"

ref: I was kidnapped from the land of the Hebrews, and here in Egypt I haven't done anything to deserve being thrown in jail.
text: A kpali bo, ba daa n gbaa mani kpeeŋtiri yi Hiburu dima paaligu. M ni lee n ta kpe ŋa gbaa, m bi niŋi yaso n daga ba yo ma dii.”
translated: And when they had brought them, they set them before the council. And the high priest questioned them, saying, "We strictly charged you not to teach in this name, yet here you have filled Jerusalem with your teaching."

ref: It is just as I said—God has shown what he intends to do.
text: “A si n niŋini maa seema ni yeli i la, naaba. Naawunni wusi i ni seema o n ya n niŋi.
translated: If you don't know something, it's not a problem, chief. Ignorance doesn't mean you can't learn it.

ref: Give them the power to collect the grain during those good years and to store it in your cities.
text: Ba deegira bindira maa yumsuma seesi n chena ŋa, a zaali a nyini naaba kpeeŋu niŋi. Ba zaali a tiŋkarigu zaa puwa.
translated: When they reached the place where the water was, he saw that the chief had already arrived. He saw that the chief's horse had died.

ref: For seven years there were big harvests of grain.
text: Yuma ayapoin, bindira daa n niŋi ga.
translated: A person came yesterday, I didn't see (him/her).

ref: But Jacob did not send Joseph's younger brother Benjamin with them; he was afraid that something might happen to him.
text: Yakubu daa n bi ti ba sunzo Benjamin paa ba gaari, a kpali, o daa n zorini dabeem maa, o ni nye daaŋu.
translated: When Yakubu saw that they had put Benjamin in their car, he was happy, he was dancing in the room, he was very happy.

ref: We're honest men, and we come from the same family—we're not spies.”
text: Ti zaa niŋini mabiluŋko, dabluŋko biisi, ti niŋini nivumansi, ti bi niŋi daatiriba.”
translated: We will see miracles, and signs, we will see visions, we will see dreams.

ref: Joseph replied: It's like I said. You're spies,
text: Yisifu yeli ba, “Wune n na ma si n yeli ya la, ya niŋini daatiriba.
translated: Joseph said to them, "I am the one I told you about, your brother Joseph."

ref: before saying to them: Since I respect God, I'll give you a chance to save your lives.
text: Dabaa ata maa daari, Yisifu daa yeli ba, “M niŋini Naawunni zora, ya niŋi seema ni ya n wusiya ŋa, ka nye ya zu.
translated: When they were about to leave, Joseph said to them, "I fear God, do this and you will live, I am not your enemy.
